findings.category: Shell Injection / Insufficient Input Validation Documentation
findings.description: Bash pseudocode commands interpolate {STORY_ID}-derived variables without explicit sanitization guards at the call sites. Upstream validation in qa.md (STORY-[0-9]+ regex) mitigates the risk, but the sanitization contract is not documented at execution points.
findings.remediation: Document the STORY-ID sanitization contract at Bash call sites. Prefer explicit path literals over derived variables in rm commands.

findings.description: Story-scoped directory paths constructed by concatenating config base paths with {STORY_ID}. Base path sourced from test-isolation.yaml without traversal guard documentation. Risk is low as STORY_ID is validated and base path is internal.
findings.remediation: Add trust annotation comment to path construction block documenting that base path is trusted-internal.

findings.description: Lock acquisition uses non-atomic remove-then-write pattern. A concurrent QA session could overwrite the lock between Remove and Write operations.
findings.remediation: Document the non-atomic nature and acceptable risk for single-developer workflows. Note atomic alternatives for CI/CD parallel execution contexts.

findings.description: Reference files loaded dynamically without integrity checks. Git version control provides sufficient integrity tracking for this internal developer tool threat model.
findings.remediation: No action required in current threat model.

findings.description: allowed-tools frontmatter uses space-separated format in ideate.md while qa.md and create-ui.md use comma-separated format. Inconsistent formatting may cause parser to apply incorrect tool restrictions.
findings.remediation: Standardize allowed-tools to comma-separated format: 'allowed-tools: Read, Glob, AskUserQuestion, Skill'

dependency_scan.reason: Markdown configuration files with no package dependencies
